description_long: This is a single-bladed axe with a wooden handle that is predominantly yellow with a red-stained end, possibly indicating paint or a protective coating. The handle shows a natural wood grain texture, with some areas appearing rough and splintered, especially near the blade. The blade itself is metallic, with a slightly curved cutting edge and a broad, wedge-shaped profile, showing signs of wear and some discoloration. The head of the axe is securely mounted to the handle, with the wood visibly wedged into the metal. There are no visible manufacturer markings or engravings on either the blade or the handle. The overall appearance suggests it is a utilitarian tool, possibly used for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A worn, yellow-and-red wooden-handled axe with a broad, metallic blade and visible wood grain and wear.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from a side angle, highlighting the yellow handle with red end, rough wood grain, and the full profile of the metallic blade.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed head-on from the blade, showing the narrow edge of the blade and the rounded red end of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the same yellow and red handle, with the blade's wedge shape and wood grain details visible.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from above, emphasizing the straight alignment of the handle and the broad, flat surface of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a round cross-section, yellow with a red-stained end, visible wood grain, and rough texture near the blade.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a slightly curved edge and a pronounced poll, no beard, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the wooden handle inserted and secured into the steel blade head.
